summary: DHH shares the blog journey of James Prudente, a developer who documented his first ten days learning Ruby on Rails. Starting without any Ruby knowledge, Prudente progressed from installation struggles to becoming a complete convert, abandoning PHP, J2EE, and ASP.NET. DHH highlights this as a representative example of how developers new to both Ruby and Rails quickly become enthusiastic adopters.
key_points: A developer with no Ruby background was able to reimplement a project in Rails within 10 days
The learning curve, while initially steep with debugging screens and pluralization rules, resolved quickly
Rails was winning converts away from PHP, J2EE, and ASP.NET simultaneously in its earliest days
DHH used community testimonials as social proof to build momentum for Rails adoption
money_quotes: It's official, Rails owns. Bye Bye PHP. Sayonara J2EE. Arrivederci ASP.NET. Hello future.
It's so tight, clean, and beautiful that I must use it for all my web development.
I've been working with Rails for 10 days now, and there is no looking back.
The more I look the more I appreciate all the thought and design that has gone into Rails.
James started out on Rails without knowing Ruby (like many others) and has been completely taken by the combination of Ruby on Rails in such a short period of time.
key_insight: Rails' earliest growth was fueled by developers who arrived knowing nothing about Ruby yet converted completely within days, a pattern DHH actively amplified to build community momentum.
